data.title: CFTC Chair Testifies on Regulatory Issues and New Investigations
data.description: CFTC Chair Mike Selig recently testified before the U.S. House Agriculture Committee. According to Foresight News, the hearing addressed several key topics, including the jurisdiction over prediction markets, a new investigation into insider trading in the oil sector, and the collaborative 'Project Crypto' initiative with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) aimed at regulating cryptocurrencies.

data.title: Solidity Developer Survey Highlights AI Usage and Trust Issues
data.description:
The Solidity team has released its 2025 developer survey, gathering responses from 1,095 developers across 87 countries. According to Foresight News, 70% of respondents are smart contract developers, 12% are auditors or security experts, and 18% are students. Half of the participants have two years or less experience with Solidity, and 49% use it daily. The top three countries represented are India with 205 respondents, Nigeria with 148, and the United States with 83. Additionally, 74% of respondents are aged between 18 and 34.

The survey reveals that 88% of users utilize AI tools at least once a month, with 58% using them daily. However, 45% express distrust in AI outputs. Developers are adopting AI tools faster than they are building trust in them. The most common AI applications are testing (61%), writing code documentation (59%), and learning code libraries (58%).

data.title: Rakuten Wallet Enables Shiba Inu Trading for Over 40 Million Users
data.description: Rakuten Wallet has introduced support for Shiba Inu (SHIB) trading against the Japanese yen (JPY), expanding access to over 40 million users within the Rakuten ecosystem. According to NS3.AI, this development allows users to convert their Rakuten points into SHIB, which can then be exchanged into Rakuten cash. This cash can be utilized for purchases via Rakuten Pay throughout Japan, enhancing the utility and accessibility of Shiba Inu for Rakuten's extensive user base.

data.title: Steak 'n Shake to Launch Bitcoin Milkshake and Boosts Bitcoin Reserve
data.description: Steak 'n Shake has announced the introduction of a limited-time Bitcoin Milkshake, set to debut on April 27 at participating locations. According to NS3.AI, this launch coincides with the Bitcoin Conference 2026. Additionally, the company has increased its Strategic Bitcoin Reserve by adding another $10 million in Bitcoin. Steak 'n Shake currently accepts Bitcoin payments via the Lightning Network at 393 locations across the United States.

data.title: Brent crude oil rose above $95 per barrel, up 2.94% on the day.
data.description: According to Gate data, Brent crude oil rose above $95 per barrel, up 2.94% on the day.

data.title: Mastercard and Crossmint to Enable AI Agents on OpenClaw for Card Purchases
data.description: Mastercard and Crossmint have announced that AI agents on OpenClaw will soon have the capability to make purchases using existing payment cards. According to NS3.AI, this development ensures that each transaction will remain under issuer controls, processed through Mastercard's network, and will require user authorization. The integration is set to begin in early access, with Lobster.cash operating on crypto rails developed in collaboration with partners such as Solana and Circle.

data.title: The ceasefire agreement in Lebanon could be announced as early as tonight (April 16) local time.
data.description: Israeli sources say a ceasefire agreement in Lebanon could be announced as early as tonight (April 16) local time. (Reuters)

data.title: South Africa Considers Extending Fuel-Tax Cut Amid Oil Shock
data.description: South Africa has the fiscal capacity to extend a fuel-tax cut by two months to mitigate the impact of an oil shock caused by the Iran war, according to Citigroup Inc. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential economic measures the country might take to support consumers facing rising fuel prices. The extension aims to provide temporary relief to South African consumers as global oil markets react to geopolitical tensions. Citigroup's analysis suggests that the government has sufficient fiscal space to implement this measure without significantly affecting its budgetary commitments. The decision comes as part of broader efforts to stabilize the economy and protect consumers from volatile energy costs.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Claude Introduces Identity Verification for Certain Users
data.description:
Claude, a prominent AI chatbot, has quietly implemented identity verification requirements for some users. According to Odaily, this measure necessitates the submission of government-issued identification and a real-time selfie when accessing specific features, conducting platform integrity checks, or fulfilling compliance measures. This marks the first instance of such verification being required by a mainstream AI chatbot.

Anthropic, the company behind Claude, stated that the verification data is solely used to confirm user identity and currently targets a small number of accounts suspected of potential fraud or violations. The identity verification process is supported by partner Persona, with data stored on Persona's servers and encrypted, ensuring it is not used for model training. This move has sparked dissatisfaction among privacy-conscious users.

data.title: U.S. President Trump to Promote Economic Agenda in Western States Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is set to embark on a tour across western U.S. states to advocate for his economic policies. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the administration's efforts to address growing discontent over the ongoing conflict with Iran. This initiative comes as the White House faces mounting pressure with the November midterm elections approaching. The tour aims to bolster support for Trump's economic agenda amidst the geopolitical tensions.

data.title: ECB's Rehn Highlights Uncertainty in Energy Shock's Inflation Impact
data.description: Olli Rehn, a member of the European Central Bank's Governing Council, emphasized the current focus on the persistent inflation effects stemming from the energy shock. According to Jin10, Rehn noted that the outlook for these impacts remains unclear.

data.title: Bank of Ireland Plans Delisting from London Stock Exchange to Cut Costs
data.description: Bank of Ireland has announced its intention to delist from the London Stock Exchange in an effort to reduce expenses. Bloomberg posted on X that the bank cited low trading volumes of its shares on the exchange as a primary reason for this decision. The move is part of a broader strategy to streamline operations and focus on more cost-effective measures. The bank's shares will continue to be traded on the Euronext Dublin, where it sees more significant trading activity. This decision reflects a growing trend among companies seeking to optimize their listing strategies in response to market conditions.

data.title: Amazon's Price Targets Reaffirmed by Major Firms
data.description: Loop Capital, Roth Capital, and Barclays have reaffirmed their price targets for Amazon, ranging from $285 to $360. According to NS3.AI, Amazon's stock was trading around $249 at the time of writing. TipRanks data indicates an average target of $284.65, based on assessments from 45 analysts.

data.title: Portugal Enters Offshore Renminbi Bond Market to Diversify Funding
data.description: Portugal has made history by becoming the first euro-area nation to issue bonds in the offshore renminbi market, securing approximately €250 million. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting this strategic move as part of Portugal's efforts to diversify its funding sources. This development marks a significant step for Portugal in exploring alternative financial avenues beyond traditional euro-denominated bonds. The decision to tap into the renminbi bond market underscores the growing importance of the Chinese currency in global finance. Portugal's initiative may pave the way for other European countries to consider similar strategies in their financial operations.

data.title: Fed Governor Milan: Treasury Yields Not a Policy Target
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Milan stated that Treasury yields are not a direct target of the Federal Reserve's policy. According to Jin10, Milan emphasized that these yields serve as a reference factor in the process of achieving the Fed's inflation and employment goals.

data.title: The US government deposited 8.2 BTC into Coinbase Prime.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, the US government deposited 8.2 BTC, worth $606,000, into Coinbase Prime. These funds originated from assets previously seized related to the Bitfinex hack.

data.title: French Police Rescue Kidnapped Family in Crypto Ransom Case
data.description: French police successfully rescued a mother and her son after they were kidnapped in a ransom plot linked to the father's cryptocurrency business. According to NS3.AI, GIGN officers freed the pair from a hotel room in Val-de-Marne on Tuesday at approximately 6 a.m., following their abduction from Burgundy the previous day. French Minister Jean-Didier Berger announced that authorities are developing a comprehensive strategy to tackle crypto-related ransom attacks in the coming weeks.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Fed Governor Milan: AI May Affect Economic Growth-Unemployment Link
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Milan has noted that the relationship between economic growth and unemployment rates is not as closely linked as it was in the past. According to Jin10, the reasons for this change are not entirely clear, but artificial intelligence could be a contributing factor.

data.title: India's Cricket Craze Attracts Billionaire Investments
data.description: Bloomberg posted on X that India's passion for cricket is attracting significant investments from billionaires. The sport's immense popularity in the country has led to increased financial interest, with investors seeking to capitalize on its widespread appeal. In other news, SpaceX is organizing site visits in preparation for its upcoming IPO, signaling a major step forward for the aerospace company. Additionally, an investor from the UAE has acquired a stake in Joe & the Juice, expanding their portfolio in the food and beverage sector.

data.title: The UK's FCA has released a final draft of its framework for crypto assets, which will bring practices such as token custody exceeding 24 hours and automated permissions under regulation.
data.description: The UK Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) has released the final draft of its crypto asset framework. The new regulations, scheduled to take effect on October 25, 2027, will bring most crypto activities under the Financial Services and Markets Act. Under the proposal, any institution holding a client's crypto assets for more than 24 hours or possessing the ability to revoke a client's rights will be considered a regulated custodian and required to hold a full guarantee license. Validators and node operators offering yield or reward reinvestment features will lose their technical exemptions. Furthermore, stablecoin issuers within the UK must control the entire lifecycle from issuance to redemption. Relevant institutions must submit authorization applications between September 30, 2026, and February 28, 2027.
